predictions.root_cause: Worker pods are pending because the analytics-batch namespace CPU quota is exhausted (limits.cpu 16/16), so Airflow tasks remain queued with zero available workers.
evidence: data/k8s/INC-001-events.yaml — FailedScheduling and quota ThresholdExceeded for analytics-batch-cpu-quota
data/metrics/INC-001.json — k8s.namespace.quota.cpu.used_percent at 100% and airflow.workers.available at 0
data/logs/INC-001.log — insufficient cpu in namespace analytics-batch and no alive workers
data/airflow/INC-001-status.json — 51 queued tasks and 11 pending worker pods
clarifying_questions: Were any namespace quota changes or large DAG backfills started in the last 24 hours?
Did any new workloads deploy into analytics-batch before tasks began queuing?
actions: Confirm quota saturation via metrics and Kubernetes events
Request CPU quota increase through change control
Pause non-critical DAGs after stakeholder approval
predictions.rollback_recommendation: No rollback recommended. Address analytics-batch CPU quota exhaustion and restore worker capacity first.
predictions.incident_summary: Multiple Airflow tasks remained queued because worker pods could not schedule in analytics-batch. Kubernetes events show FailedScheduling and CPU quota at limits.cpu 16/16. Metrics confirm 100% quota usage and zero available workers. The scheduler is operational but cannot place workers. Remediation requires quota increase through change control or deprioritizing non-critical DAGs after approval.
predictions.uncertainty: High confidence given aligned quota, scheduling, and worker availability signals.

predictions.root_cause: DAG customer_segmentation_daily fails after deploy v2.14.0 due to missing BQ_DATASET_PROD and invalid BigQuery dataset analytics_prod_v2.
evidence: data/logs/INC-002.log — KeyError BQ_DATASET_PROD not set and dataset analytics_prod_v2 not found
data/metrics/INC-002.json — task failure rate spike after deploy
clarifying_questions: Can deployment v2.14.0 be rolled back to v2.13.2 safely?
actions: Rollback to DAG bundle v2.13.2 via release process
Add BQ_DATASET_PROD to worker deployment configuration
Re-run failed DAG after staging validation
predictions.rollback_recommendation: Rollback DAG bundle from v2.14.0 to v2.13.2 via standard release process, or hotfix missing BQ_DATASET_PROD and correct dataset name.
predictions.incident_summary: DAG customer_segmentation_daily began failing immediately after v2.14.0 deployment. Logs show missing BQ_DATASET_PROD and invalid dataset analytics_prod_v2. Task failure rate spiked post-deploy. Rollback or config hotfix required before downstream BI recovers.
predictions.uncertainty: Confident about deploy correlation; may need config diff review to confirm dataset naming standard.

predictions.root_cause: Airflow scheduler is restarting frequently because metadata database connections are failing; logs mention pool pressure but root cause may need more DB review.
evidence: data/logs/INC-003.log — too many connections and metadata DB connection pool exhausted
data/airflow/INC-003-status.json — scheduler_healthy false with 18 restarts noted
actions: Restart all scheduler pods immediately to clear errors
Review scheduler logs for connection errors
predictions.rollback_recommendation: Rollback scheduler deployment to previous version to restore stability.
predictions.incident_summary: Scheduler is restarting due to database connection errors. Further investigation needed.
predictions.uncertainty: Partial evidence — PgBouncer metrics not yet cited; need DBA confirmation on pool saturation.
